draft:
Dear Lumiq Team,

I hope this email finds you well. 

I am writing to address the recent major production downtime caused by the interruption in the Hermes Classification API. We understand the significant impact this outage had on your operations, resulting in idling labor and a backlog of unclassified shipments.

Our team is currently conducting a thorough root-cause analysis (RCA) to identify the source of the issue and prevent future occurrences. We sincerely apologize for any inconvenience this may have caused and are committed to resolving this matter promptly.

In accordance with our Enterprise SLA guidelines, we will work closely with you to determine appropriate compensation for the incurred losses. Our aim is to ensure that such incidents are minimized in the future, and we value your partnership and trust in us.

Please rest assured that we are taking this matter seriously and will keep you updated on our findings and proposed solutions.

Thank you for bringing this to our attention, and we appreciate your understanding and patience as we work to address this issue.

Best regards,

[Your Name]
Hermes Team
original_subject: CRITICAL: Major Production Downtime due to API Interruption
original_body:
Hi Hermes Team,
I am writing to express my severe disappointment with the service reliability today. We experienced a complete interruption of the Hermes Classification API between 2:00 PM and 3:30 PM EST.
This API is integrated directly into our real-time manufacturing dispatch line. Because of the outage, our sorting systems ground to a halt, resulting in 90 minutes of idling labor and a backlog of over 4,000 unclassified shipments. This downtime has cost us roughly $25,000 in immediate logistical delays.
We require an immediate root-cause analysis (RCA) and want to know how you plan to compensate us under our Enterprise SLA guidelines.
Regards,
Derek Vaughn
VP of Global Operations, GlobalFab Logistics
